title: The Systems Renaissance
description: Explore the evolution of systems programming from C to the modern era of memory safety and zero-cost abstractions.

steps.challenge: Identify how C's philosophy of 'trusting the programmer' led to both its success and its legacy of security challenges.
Examine RAII (Resource Acquisition Is Initialization) and how it attempted to solve resource management manually.
Analyze the 'Borrow Checker' concept. How does it provide memory safety without a garbage collector?
Explore 'Comptime'. How does it differ from C++ templates or Rust macros?

title: The Functional Frontier
description: Trace the lineage of declarative and functional programming from the lambda calculus to modern type systems.

steps.challenge: Understand 'Homoiconicity'. Why is it powerful to treat code as data?
Investigate 'Lazy Evaluation' and 'Monads'. How do they manage side effects in a pure environment?
The Elm Architecture (TEA). How does it influence modern React/Redux patterns?
Examine 'Persistent Data Structures' and how they enable efficient immutability.

title: The Web Explosion
description: Follow the rapid evolution of the web from simple scripting to complex, type-safe applications.

steps.challenge: Prototype-based inheritance. How does it differ from class-based inheritance?
The 'Shared Nothing' architecture. How did it help PHP scale the early web?
Structural vs. Nominal typing. Why was structural typing chosen for TS?
How does Wasm change the definition of a 'Web Language'?
